plan.title: Swim — Durable Execution State Machine for Waveplan
notes: Coarse-grained objective tree. One unit per phase (TN.1). Sub-decomposition lands in a follow-up plan after this tree is approved.
Strict serial waves: each phase consumes outputs of the prior phase.
Locked decisions before wave generation (Sigma proposals + step_id fix), all enforced in T1.1:
1. Keep `status` field in schedule rows during transition; mark deprecated; drop in a future phase behind feature flag.
3. Keep `wp_invoke` as display/debug-only; never an execution source; runner must read `invoke.argv` exclusively.
4. Default swim artifact layout: `.waveplan/swim/<plan-basename>/` containing `swim.lock`, `logs/`, and journal sidecar.
5. step_id separator switches to underscore: `S1_T1.1_implement` (parser-trivial, avoids period collision with task IDs like `T1.1`).
Dagdir guardrails apply globally: no planner logic, no dialect parsing, no DAG mutation in swim.
Phase 5 (MCP) marked optional in the spec but included as T5.1 to keep CLI/MCP parity tracked.

T1.title: Phase 1 — Contracts and compiler hardening (schedule v2 + locked decisions)

T2.title: Phase 2 — Swim core + journal engine + race-closure protocol

T3.title: Phase 3 — Step runner, logging, dry-run, --apply / run --until

T4.title: Phase 4 — CLI swim command group + ops documentation

T5.title: Phase 5 — MCP swim tooling + CLI/MCP parity

T1.1.title: Emit schedule v2 from wp-emit-wave-execution.sh: schema_version, step_id (underscore-separated S{wave}_{task_id}_{action}), seq, action, requires, produces, invoke.argv canonical, wp_invoke debug-only, status field kept with deprecation notice; deterministic byte-equivalent output; publish swim-schedule-schema-v2.json and swim-journal-schema-v1.json; add Go-side strict validators

T2.1.title: Swim engine: next-step resolver over schedule + journal + live state; precondition evaluator (requires) and postcondition validator (produces) including review_taken timestamp derivation; append-only journal with file-lock CAS on cursor; apply-time race-closure protocol (snapshot tokens A/B before invoke, C after); unknown-recovery on crash between invoke and journal append

T3.1.title: Swim runner: execute invoke.argv via argv (no shell eval); per-step stdout/stderr log files under .waveplan/swim/<plan-basename>/logs/; dry-run mode emits intended command + gating decision without state mutation; --apply transaction holds .waveplan/swim/<plan-basename>/swim.lock; run --until <action|seq:N|step:ID> with stop-on-failure

T4.1.title: waveplan-cli swim command group: compile/next/step/run/journal subcommands; default read-only, --apply gated; --ack-unknown override; failure recovery and manual-override workflow documented in 2026-05-05-swim-ops.md; README updated with examples

T5.1.title: MCP tools waveplan_swim_compile / _next / _step / _run / _journal mirroring CLI surface; deterministic JSON returns (no mixed text payloads); contract parity tests CLI vs MCP
